title: Java News Roundup: Spring Tools, Helidon, Open Liberty, TomEE, JobRunr, Hibernate, Commonhaus
gist: InfoQ's June 15 2026 Java roundup covers releases (Spring Tools, Helidon, Open Liberty, TomEE 11, Hibernate 8 beta, JobRunr, Gradle 9.6, JDK 27/28 EA), Quarkus emergency CVE patches, an experimental Claude Code plugin in Spring Tools, and four OkHttp/Retrofit-family projects joining Commonhaus after leaving Block.

themes.theme: AI tooling entering the Java ecosystem

insights.claim: Spring Tools 5.2.0 ships a new experimental Claude Code Plugin that provides an embedded MCP server and Claude code skills, plus support for Spring AI, signaling AI-coding integration moving into mainstream Java IDE tooling.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: An embedded MCP server and Claude skills in Spring Tools shows MCP and Claude Code spreading into the established enterprise Java stack — supports MCP-standardization thesis.
insights.quote: a new, experimental Claude Code Plugin that provides an embedded MCP server and Claude code skills

insights.claim: Quarkus issued emergency maintenance releases across five release trains (3.20, 3.27, 3.33, 3.36, 3.37) to address CVE-2026-50559, a vulnerability related to CVE-2026-39852 where attackers use encoded semicolons to smuggle matrix parameters past the security layer and encoded slashes/backslashes to access protected static resources.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Active exploitation-class path-traversal CVEs in a popular framework underscore ongoing JVM supply-chain risk requiring rapid patching.

insights.claim: Open Liberty 26.0.0.6 resolved CVE-2026-4410 (a DoS via memory exhaustion from a crafted request) and CVE-2026-5516 (a timing-window authentication bypass under limited conditions), affecting a broad range of prior Open Liberty versions.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: An authentication-bypass CVE in an enterprise app server is a patch-priority signal for affected Java shops.

insights.claim: OkHttp, Okio, Retrofit, and SQLDelight — formerly under Block (formerly Square) — joined the Commonhaus Foundation, with maintainers Jesse Wilson and Jake Wharton citing the need for dependable, stable governance after leaving Block.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Widely-used Android/Java HTTP libraries moving to a neutral foundation reduces single-company dependency risk for the broad ecosystem relying on them.
insights.quote: Our users have a lot of trust in us and we want to show them that our projects' new home is dependable.

insights.claim: Hibernate ORM 8.0 first beta implements Jakarta Persistence 4.0-M5, adds graph-based flush coordination that plans insert/update/delete/collection work using mapped relational constraints, and supports row-level security to enforce discriminator-based multi-tenancy on PostgreSQL, Db2, SQL Server, and CockroachDB.
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Row-level-security-backed multi-tenancy in the dominant Java ORM eases secure SaaS data isolation — relevant to multi-tenant architecture choices.

insights.claim: Gradle 9.6.0 GA improves Configuration Cache hit rates by accurately tracking project properties from system properties and environment variables, adds a --non-interactive CLI option for automated environments, and deprecates Groovy DSL implicit property/method lookup in parent projects (to be removed in Gradle 10.0).
insights.speaker: Michael Redlich
insights.so_what: Incremental build-tool maturation; the --non-interactive flag and config-cache gains matter for CI/automation and agentic build environments.
